The partition of India in 1947 led to the creation of two independent dominions, India and Pakistan. The Vijayanagara Empire successfully resisted Islamic invasions in South India for over two centuries. Chandragupta Maurya founded the Maurya Empire by overthrowing the Nanda Dynasty. Akbar the Great is remembered for his policies of religious tolerance and centralized administration. The Mughal Empire, established in 1526, brought significant changes to Indian architecture and administration. The Harappan civilization is known for its impressive urban planning and drainage systems. Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi became a symbol of resistance during the Indian Rebellion of 1857. The Swadeshi movement encouraged the boycott of British goods to promote Indian industries. The Indian Rebellion of 1857 was a major uprising against the rule of the British East India Company. The Chola Dynasty was one of the longest-ruling dynasties in the history of southern India. The Indus Valley Civilization was one of the world's earliest major urban settlements. The Indian National Congress, founded in 1885, was the first modern nationalist movement to emerge in the British Empire. The Jallianwala Bagh massacre of 1919 deeply shocked the nation and catalyzed the freedom struggle. The Battle of Plassey in 1757 established company rule in Bengal. The Gupta Empire is often described as the Golden Age of India due to extensive inventions and discoveries. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the principal architect of the Constitution of India. Mahatma Gandhi led the non-violent freedom movement that eventually resulted in India's independence in 1947. The Quit India Movement was a civil disobedience movement launched by Gandhi in 1942.
